question: How to start my jetski in different situations?
content:
1. For every engine start, operate only in an open area. Turn the fuel cock knob to ON; use RES only if you run out of fuel, refuel as soon as possible, then return it to ON. Attach the engine shut-off cord to your left wrist and install the clip under the engine shut-off switch; the engine will not start if this clip is removed.<PIC>
2. For a cold engine, use the choke lever: direction a turns the choke on, and direction b turns it off.<PIC>
3. Press the green start switch to start the engine.<PIC> After the engine starts, check that cooling water is discharged from the pilot outlet. If the cooling passages are dry, water may take about 20 seconds to reach the outlet; if no water discharges, stop the engine and check the cause.
4. Starting/boarding in shallow water: do not start in less than 60 cm (2 ft) of water. Move the watercraft to deep enough water, board from the side or rear, attach the lanyard and clip, grip the handlebars, put both feet in the footwell, then start and move off.<PIC>
5. Starting from a dock: board from the side, attach the lanyard and clip, push the watercraft away from the dock, grip the handlebars, put both feet in the footwell, then start and move off.<PIC>
6. Boarding alone from the water: climb onto the rear platform, move to the seat, attach the lanyard and clip, grip the handlebars, put both feet in the footwell, start the engine, then look in all directions before starting off.
7. With a passenger: do not apply throttle until the passenger is seated, has both feet in the footwell, and is holding on to the operator. Start only after the passenger is secure, look in all directions, then accelerate to planing speed. If balancing at a standstill is difficult, have the passenger steady the craft while the operator boards and balances it, then start the engine and keep it at idle while the passenger climbs aboard before accelerating to planing speed.<PIC>
8. Before starting off in any situation, make sure there are no boats, obstructions, or swimmers around you. The watercraft has no brakes; do not release the throttle when trying to steer away from objects because you need throttle to steer.

5. Starting from a dock: board from the side, attach the lanyard and clip, push the watercraft away from the dock, grip the handlebars, put both feet in the footwell, then start and move off.<PIC>

Start only after the passenger is secure, look in all directions, then accelerate to planing speed. If balancing at a standstill is difficult, have the passenger steady the craft while the operator boards and balances it, then start the engine and keep it at idle while the passenger climbs aboard before accelerating to planing speed.<PIC>

The watercraft has no brakes; do not release the throttle when trying to steer away from objects because you need throttle to steer.",["Manual20_44", "Manual20_46", "Manual20_48", "Manual20_88", "Manual20_89", "jetski_06"]
notes: Answer combines the general engine-start controls with the manual's shallow-water, dock, solo-boarding, and passenger-start procedures. Images are used only for the shut-off clip, choke, start switch, shallow-water depth limit, dock departure, and passenger balancing setup.
